overview.text: Dark Noise’s subscription base is essentially flat, with active subscriptions inching from 2,526 to 2,536 (+0.4%) and MRR holding at $4,562 (‑0.03%). Revenue is slipping for the third straight 28‑day window, down 0.9% to $4,919 and now 7.8% above MRR, indicating one‑off or annual purchases are masking a structural decline. The funnel shows healthy top‑of‑funnel growth (new trials +16% to 281) but poor conversion—only 0.45 transactions per new customer (627 transactions for 1,401 new users).

revenue.text: Revenue fell 0.9% over the last 28 days to $4,919, marking the third straight decline ( $5,148 → $4,984 → $4,939). The 28‑day revenue exceeds MRR by 7.8% ($4,919 vs $4,562), indicating a sizable non‑recurring component—likely one‑time purchases or annual upfront payments—driving cash above the stable recurring base.

mrr.text: MRR held steady at $4,562, a negligible -0.03% change, while daily average MRR slid from $4,540 to $4,539, indicating new subscriptions are being offset by churn. Subscription movement shows a net gain of only 7 accounts (2526 → 2536) despite 116 new sign‑ups and 16 reactivations, because 125 churned customers erased most of the inflow. This flat MRR amid minimal net growth signals a structural revenue decline that must be addressed.

churn.text: Churn remains a drag: 125 subscriptions left in the last 28 days, offset only by 116 new sign‑ups and 16 reactivations, yielding a net gain of just 7 active subs. MRR is essentially flat (−0.0% over the period) and revenue has slipped three periods in a row, indicating that retention is not improving despite a healthy inflow of new trials (up 16%).

subscribers.text: The subscriber base grew marginally, rising from 2,526 to 2,536 active subscriptions (+7 net) despite 125 churned accounts, offset by 116 new sign‑ups and 16 reactivations. New customers now represent 55% of the total base (1,401 of 2,536), yet only 0.45 transactions per new customer (627 transactions) indicate low early monetization, while active trials have climbed 16% to 67, feeding the pipeline.

trials.text: Trial activation is up 16% (281 new trials vs 242 prior) but only 67 remain active, yielding an estimated 178 conversions out of 281 trials (≈63% conversion). However, with just 0.45 transactions per new customer (627 transactions/1,401 new customers) the funnel leaks heavily after trial, indicating most trialists are not monetizing beyond the free period. Immediate focus on post‑trial onboarding and pricing nudges is required to lift the low transaction rate.

brief.text: Active trials rose 16% to 67, but conversion remains low—only 0.45 transactions per new customer and an estimated 178 of 281 new trials have yet to convert. Revenue slipped 0.9% to $4,919 and has declined three periods in a row, while MRR is flat, indicating new sign‑ups are being offset by churn (125 lost vs 116 new). The paid base grew marginally (+7) despite churn, suggesting acquisition is outpacing retention but not driving recurring growth. Immediate action: launch a targeted in‑app upsell/retention flow for trial users and recent sign‑ups to boost conversion and stem the revenue downtrend.
